lesson.title: Lesson 15: Fifty Pence Worth of Trouble

rights.notes: Migrated from legacy published content; migration does not establish redistribution rights.

segments.text: Lesson 15.

translation.text: 第15课。

segments.text: Fifty Pence Worth of Trouble.

translation.text: 五十便士惹来的麻烦。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translation.text: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: Did George get anything for his fifty pence?

translation.text: 乔治用他的五十便士得到什么了吗？

segments.text: What?

translation.text: 是什么？

segments.text: always appreciate small gifts of money.

translation.text: 孩子们总是很喜欢收到一点零钱。

segments.text: Mom or dad, of course, provides a regular supply of pocket money, but uncles and aunts are always a source of extra income.

translation.text: 当然，爸爸妈妈会定期给零花钱，但叔叔阿姨也总是额外收入的来源。

segments.text: With some children, small sums go a long way.

translation.text: 对有些孩子来说，一点钱可以用很久。

segments.text: If fifty pence pieces are not exchanged for sweets, they rattle for months inside money boxes.

translation.text: 如果五十便士硬币没有拿去换糖果，它们会在储蓄罐里叮当作响好几个月。

segments.text: Only very thrifty children manage to fill up a money box.

translation.text: 只有非常节俭的孩子才能把储蓄罐装满。

segments.text: For most of them, fifty pence is a small price to pay for a nice big bar of chocolate.

translation.text: 对大多数孩子来说，花五十便士买一大块美味巧克力并不算贵。

segments.text: My nephew, George, has a money box, but it is always empty.

translation.text: 我的外甥乔治有一个储蓄罐，但它总是空的。

segments.text: Very few of the fifty pence pieces and pound coins I have given him have found their way there.

translation.text: 我给过他的五十便士和一英镑硬币，几乎没有几枚进过那个储蓄罐。

segments.text: I gave him fifty pence yesterday and advised him to save it.

translation.text: 昨天我给了他五十便士，并劝他存起来。

segments.text: Instead, he bought himself fifty pence worth of trouble.

translation.text: 可他反而用这五十便士给自己买来了一场麻烦。

segments.text: On his way to the sweet shop, he dropped his fifty pence and it bounced along the pavement and then disappeared down a drain.

translation.text: 在去糖果店的路上，他把五十便士掉在地上，硬币沿人行道滚了几下，随后掉进了下水道。

segments.text: George took off his jacket, rolled up his sleeves, and pushed his right arm through the drain cover.

translation.text: 乔治脱下夹克，卷起袖子，把右臂伸进下水道盖。

segments.text: He could not find his fifty pence piece anywhere, and what is more, he could not get his arm out.

translation.text: 他到处都找不到那枚五十便士；更糟的是，他的胳膊也拔不出来了。

segments.text: A crowd of people gathered round him and a lady rubbed his arm with soap and butter, but George was firmly stuck.

translation.text: 一群人围在他身边，一位女士用肥皂和黄油涂抹他的手臂，但乔治仍被牢牢卡住。

segments.text: The fire brigade was called and two firefighters freed George using a special type of grease.

translation.text: 人们叫来了消防队，两名消防员用一种特制润滑油救出了乔治。

segments.text: George was not too upset by his experience because the lady who owns the sweet shop heard about his troubles and rewarded him with a large box of chocolates.

translation.text: 乔治并没有因这次经历过分沮丧，因为糖果店的女店主听说他的遭遇后，奖给他一大盒巧克力。
